extraction.one_line_summary: LAPORTE Christophe won after VINGEGAARD Jonas led him out in final kilometer to catch solo escapee HAYTER Ethan meters from the line.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD Jonas sacrificed GC positioning by leading out LAPORTE Christophe in final kilometer, reversing traditional team hierarchy.
HAYTER Ethan caught 200m from line despite extended solo effort; hilly sprints require breakaway attacks before 1km-to-go.
JORGENSON Matteo finished second as pure sprinter despite 9000ft climbing, revealing climbing tolerance matters more than pure power.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Willing to expend GC energy for team stage wins, showing extraordinary tactical flexibility and team cohesion.
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: Absorbed 9000ft climbing in rain then delivered finishing speed, confirming ability to win hard semi-sprint stages.
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: neutral: Strong enough to survive solo but looked back repeatedly final kilometer, revealing psychological uncertainty under pressure.
rider_signals.JORGENSON Matteo: positive: Second place on 9000ft stage signals exceptional climbing ability for sprinter-type, changes Tour stage selection calculus.
rider_signals.GAUDU David: neutral: Third at Paris-Nice but unremarkable here; prior form may not translate when VINGEGAARD peaks specifically for objectives.
